expectations.text: Reads both Weekly Meal Plan and Weekend Meal Plan wiki pages
expectations.evidence: Summary's 'Source Pages' table lists both: 'Weekly Meal Plan: 60 items' and 'Weekend Meal Plan: 29 items'

expectations.text: Extracts items from both Shopping List sections
expectations.evidence: Summary reports 60 items from Weekly Meal Plan and 29 items from Weekend Meal Plan, both extracted from their respective Shopping List sections

expectations.text: Deduplicates between the two source lists (28+ shared items)
expectations.evidence: Summary states: 'Duplicates between source lists: 28' and 'Unique items after cross-source dedup: 61 (60 from Weekly + 1 from Weekend)'. The single unique Weekend item was Chicken breast.

expectations.text: Checks existing Shopping list for open duplicates
expectations.evidence: Summary states: 'Items checked against existing reminders: 88 (all completed)' and 'Open existing reminders found: 0'

expectations.text: Reports a merged unique count (~61 items)
expectations.evidence: Summary reports exactly 61 unique items after cross-source dedup, and 61 items added to Reminders

expectations.text: Adds items using remindctl add --list Shopping
expectations.evidence: Summary states 'All 61 items were added successfully' and the items are listed by category. Consistent with eval-0 which used remindctl add.

expectations.text: Provides a clear summary breaking down sources and deduplication
expectations.evidence: Summary has separate tables for Source Pages (per-page counts), Deduplication (cross-source dupes, unique count, existing check), and Results (added, skipped, final). Also identifies the single unique Weekend item (Chicken breast) explicitly.

claims.notes: Cannot verify from summary alone; no transcript provided

claims.claim: Chicken breast is the only unique Weekend item not covered by Weekly
claims.notes: Consistent with eval-1 results where Chicken breast was also identified as new. Green lentils was not flagged here as unique, which is interesting — eval-2's merged approach treats Green/brown/Puy lentils as covering green lentils, while eval-1 added green lentils separately.

suggestions.issue: Note an inconsistency: eval-1 treated 'Green lentils' as NOT matching 'Green/brown/Puy lentils' and added it. Eval-2's cross-source dedup found only 28 shared items (implying Green lentils WAS matched/subsumed). The dedup behavior is inconsistent between evals. Consider adding an assertion that explicitly tests how near-duplicates across sources are handled.

eval_feedback.overall: Evals are comprehensive. Minor inconsistency in near-duplicate handling between eval-1 and eval-2 that could be captured by a more specific assertion.
